file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: If you have acute coronary syndrome, you have one or more conditions caused by a blockage of blood flow to your heart muscle. This is a medical emergency because you may be having a heart attack, a condition in which your heart muscle begins to die.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: Your heart is a muscular organ that pumps blood containing the oxygen and nutrients your body needs. The main pumping chamber of your heart is the left ventricle. When your left ventricle contracts, it sends oxygen-rich blood to your body through a large artery called the aorta.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: Connected to your aorta are small arteries called coronary arteries. Blood flows from your aorta through the coronary arteries to supply your heart muscle with oxygen and nutrients.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: If you have acute coronary syndrome, blood flow through your coronary arteries is severely reduced or completely blocked. One possible cause of reduced blood flow is atherosclerosis. In this condition, a build-up of a fatty substance called plaque can narrow your coronary If this plaque ruptures, a blood clot can form and block the artery. A blood clot is the most common cause of coronary artery blockage.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: Other less common causes of reduced blood flow include coronary artery spasm or dissection. In a coronary artery spasm, triggers such as drugs, smoking, cold weather, and extreme stress or emotions can cause a temporary and sudden tightening of a coronary artery.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: During a coronary artery dissection, the inside wall of one of your coronary arteries separates which can block blood flow.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: Regardless of the cause, a blockage in either coronary artery prevents the oxygen and nutrients in your blood from reaching the part of your heart supplied by the artery. As a result, heart muscle in that area starts to die. Death of part of your heart muscle is called a heart attack. It's also known as a myocardial infarction or MI.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: A blocked coronary artery may also cause you to feel sudden pain, discomfort, tightening, or a burning sensation in your chest called angina. This pain may extend to your upper abdomen, shoulders, arms, neck, and lower jaw. If you have angina when you're at rest or frequent angina that prevents even moderate physical activity, you have unstable angina which is the main symptom of acute coronary

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: Other symptoms of acute coronary syndrome include shortness of breath, dizziness, nausea, and sweating.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: If you've had a heart attack or have other types of acute coronary syndrome, your doctor may prescribe oxygen therapy to get more oxygen into your blood. You may take aspirin or other prescription blood thinner drugs to prevent blood clots.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: Thrombolytics, also known as clot buster drugs, may be used to break up any existing blood

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: Cards such as nitroglycerin and morphine will relax your coronary arteries and relieve the pain of angina. You may also receive drugs called beta blockers that slow down your heart and reduce its need for oxygen.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: Your doctor may also recommend immediate surgical procedures such as coronary angioplasty, in which a balloon-tipped catheter inflates inside your blocked coronary artery to open it. After inflating, the balloon catheter may leave behind a mesh-like device called a stent to hold your artery open.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: Or you may have a coronary artery bypass graft or cabbage. Cigilage is a surgical procedure in which the blocked areas of the coronary arteries are bypassed with veins or artificial graft material.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: Seek treatment immediately if you have the symptoms of acute coronary syndrome.

file: ANH14119_Varicocele Repair.mp4
title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: A varicoseal is a group of swollen blood vessels called veins in a man's scrotum.

file: ANH14119_Varicocele Repair.mp4
title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: The male reproductive system includes the testicles, also known as testes, which are two glands inside a pouch of skin called the scrotum. The testicles normally produce and store reproductive cells called sperm. They also produce a hormone called testosterone. Testosterone is important for sperm production, muscle strength, and sex drive.

file: ANH14119_Varicocele Repair.mp4
title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: The scrotum is outside the body, which keeps the testicles cool enough to make sperm. Each testicle is suspended inside the scrotum by a structure called the spermatic cord. Inside each cord are blood vessels, and a tube that sperm travel through called the vas deferens.

file: ANH14119_Varicocele Repair.mp4
title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: The testicular artery is the blood vessel that brings most of the blood to the testicle. Most blood leaves the testicle through a group of veins called the Pampiniform Plexus. The blood also leaves the testicle through smaller veins next to the vas deferens that bypass the Pampiniform Plexus. From the Pampiniform Plexus, blood flows into the testicular veins.

file: ANH14119_Varicocele Repair.mp4
title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: A series of one-way valves inside the testicular veins prevent blood from flowing backward into the testicles.

file: ANH14119_Varicocele Repair.mp4
title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: A varicoseal may begin to form when the valves inside the testicular vein are damaged or don't work properly. The valves may not close completely, allowing blood to flow backward inside the vein to the testicle.

file: ANH14119_Varicocele Repair.mp4
title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: In addition, since the left testicular vein is straighter and longer than the right testicular vein, there is a greater downward pressure of the blood inside the left vein.

file: ANH14119_Varicocele Repair.mp4
title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: Another problem may occur where your left testicular vein connects to a larger vein coming from your kidney, called the left renal vein. An artery to the intestines may squeeze the left renal vein, causing blood to back up in the left testicular vein. This is called nutcracker syndrome.

file: ANH14119_Varicocele Repair.mp4
title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: As a result of any of these problems, a varicoseal can form in the left Pampiniform Plexus when blood begins to back up in it. The blood pooling in these veins causes them to become swollen.

file: ANH14119_Varicocele Repair.mp4
title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: Depending on the severity of the varicoseal, the pooled blood may raise the temperature around the testicle. This higher temperature can make it harder for the testicle to produce sperm. This condition may decrease male fertility, which is the ability to get a woman pregnant.

file: ANH14119_Varicocele Repair.mp4
title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: Over time, a varicoseal may also decrease the level of testosterone made in the testicles. Low testosterone can lead to decreased energy and muscle strength, as well as sexual problems.

file: ANH14119_Varicocele Repair.mp4
title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: A varicoseal doesn't usually cause symptoms or require treatment. However, if a varicoseal causes pain, discomfort, or problems with fertility, the most common treatment is either an in-winnel or sub-in-winnel microsurgical varicoselectomy.

file: ANH14119_Varicocele Repair.mp4
title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: To begin, the surgeon will make a small incision low on the groin for a sub-in-winnel varicoselectomy, or slightly higher for an in-winnel varicoselectomy.

file: ANH14119_Varicocele Repair.mp4
title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: Then, the spermatic cord will be pulled out through the incision. The surgeon will open the outer tissue of the spermatic cord and separate the varicoseal from the rest of the tissue.

file: ANH14119_Varicocele Repair.mp4
title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: After these veins are cut, blood will still be able to leave the testicle through the smaller veins on the vas deferens. These veins are less likely to become swollen.
